bead.title: Escalate repeated identical pre-claim warn fingerprints to operator attention
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Warn-only pre-claim intake failures can repeat forever with the same fingerprint across distinct ready beads. The worker looks alive because it keeps emitting `loop.idle`, but a full ready queue can make no progress and the operator only sees the failure by tailing the agent-loop log.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The warn path emits `pre_claim_intake.warn` for intake infrastructure failures in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1888-1901` and for `PreClaimIntakeError` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1947-1960`, while `appendPreClaimIntakeWarning` persists warn-only events in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4491-4510`. These paths do not maintain a per-fingerprint consecutive counter across bead IDs, so the same infrastructure/schema fault can be treated as independent warn-only noise forever.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a per-worker repeated-warning guard for `pre_claim_intake.warn` events. When the same fingerprint occurs N consecutive times across distinct bead IDs, default N=5, emit a `loop.operator_attention` event and a durable operator-attention signal that includes the fingerprint, count, distinct bead IDs, reason, and one example payload/detail. The guard must not convert ordinary single-bead readiness warnings into hard failures.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change verdict decoding, shared schema files, claim-success-rate warnings, README documentation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not park unrelated beads merely because one bead has a normal readiness finding. Do not rely on external Fizeau logs as fixtures. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
None.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail` or a new focused `TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold` injects the same pre-claim warn fingerprint for at least 5 consecutive attempts across distinct bead IDs and asserts a `loop.operator_attention` signal with fingerprint, count, distinct bead IDs, reason, and example detail.
2. `TestWorkWatch_SystemicPreClaimErrorIdlesWithoutCooldown` continues to prove non-threshold single or transient pre-claim warn outcomes do not cooldown or permanently park ordinary ready beads.
3. The default threshold is 5 and is configurable in the work loop runtime or CLI without changing existing default behavior for unrelated warnings.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail|TestWorkWatch_SystemicPreClaimErrorIdlesWithoutCooldown|TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
